Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 242 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

⸢ana⸣ ḪUL A.ŠA₃ zi-qi₂-ip ⸢giš⸣[GIŠIMMAR?] ana LU₂ la₃ TEe

(o 1) For the evil of a field (and) the shoot of a date palm not to approach a man, the apotropaic ritual:

o 22

NAM.BUR₂.BI 4!?(5) giURI₃.⸢GAL⸣ DU₃uš

(o 2) You make 4 standards.

o 33

ESIR IM.BABBAR u₃ GA₂×GIŠ giš[EREN] SU₃-šu-nu-ti

(o 3) You sprinkle them with bitumen, gypsum and cedar oil.

o 44

ina TU₁₅ 4(AŠ)-BA ša gišKIRI₆ [x A₂.GU₂].ZI tu-zaq-qap-šu₂-nu-te

(o 4) [...] the morning you erect them in the direction of the four cardinal points of the garden.

o 55

ina A₂.U₄.TE ina tu₁₅⸢MAR⸣.[TU] ⸢GI⸣.DU₈ ana ⸢d⸣40

(o 5) In the evening [...] an offering table for Ea and Asalluhi in the direction of the west, you put down [...] of the garden (and) of the field.

o 66

u₃ dASAR.LU₂.ḪI [... giš?]⸢KIRI₆?⸣ A.ŠA₃ [...] ⸢GAR⸣an

o 77

nindaKA.⸢DU NINDA.I₃.DE₂.A LAL₃⸣ [I₃].⸢NUN⸣.NA ⸢GARan⸣

(o 7) You put down kukku-cake (and) mersu-cake (made) of honey (and) butter.

o 88

[x dugLA].⸢ḪA⸣.AN SAḪAR₂-MEŠ [x x] ⸢SA₅⸣-ma ⸢GARan⸣

(o 8) You fill [...] porous flasks [...] and put (them) down.

o 99

[x dug]⸢A⸣.DA.GUR₅ GUBan [x x] ⸢šim⸣LI ⸢GARan⸣

(o 9) You set up [...] libation vessels. You put down [...] (full) of juniper.

o 1010

[GIŠ?].⸢KUN⸣ BALqi₂ ninda⸢DIM₁₀⸣.MA nindaGIŠ.ŠUB.BA ⸢KI⸣ giURI₃.⸢GAL?⸣

(o 10) You lay out a haunch of meat. You pile up pannigu-bread (and) tilpānu-bread by the standards.

o 1111

ta-⸢tab?⸣-bak 4 GIN₂-TA.AM₃ [x x] GA₂×GIŠ SAG GA gišGEŠTIN ⸢LAL₃⸣ I₃.UDU KAŠ SAG

(o 11) You bring together equally 4 shekels each of [...], first-class oil, milk, wine, honey, animal fat (and) first-class beer.

o 1212

TEŠ₂.BI tuš-te-em-mid e-⸢ma⸣ KEŠ₂ IGI 4-GAL₂.LA.TA.AM₃

(o 12) You lay out a quarter at each ritual assemblage and you speak as follows.

o 1313

BALqi₂-ma ki-am ta-⸢qab-bi⸣

o 1414

DINGIR-MEŠ ra-bi₂-u₃-tu₄ EN-⸢MEŠ⸣ AN u KI at-tu-nu-[ma]

(o 14) Great gods, you are the lords of heaven and earth.

o 1515

ki-ṣir ŠA₃-ku-nu DU₈-MEŠ ia-a-ši ru-ṣa!(A-)a-⸢ni⸣

(o 15) Relax your anger. Rush to help me.

Reverse
r 1r 1

KA.INIM.MA <KEŠ₂> DU₈ra giURI₃.GAL-⸢MEŠ⸣ ša₂ tu-za-⸢qi₂⸣-[pu]

(r 1) Incantation. You clear away the ritual assemblages. You throw into a watercourse the standards that you erected, the pannigu-bread, the tilpānu-bread (and) the mersu-cake, and he should not travel the road on which he (originally) travelled. The apotropaic ritual for the evil is the same.

r 22

nindaDIM₁₀.MA-MEŠ nindaGIŠ.⸢ŠUB.BA-MEŠ⸣ NINDA.I₃.DE₂.A ana ⸢ID₂ ŠUB⸣-[ma]

r 33

KASKAL DU la₃ DU NAM.BUR₂.BI ana ⸢ḪUL⸣ ŠU.BI.AŠ.AM₃

r 44

⸢NAM⸣.BUR₂.BI ḪUL KIŠIₓ(DAG.KISIM₅×(LU.GIR₂))-MEŠ ša₂ ina E₂ LU₂ in-nam-⸢ru⸣

(r 4) Apotropaic ritual for the evil of ants that have appeared in the house of a man, for its evil not to approach the man and his house, its ritual:

r 55

⸢ḪUL⸣-šu₂ ana NA u E₂-šu₂ la₃ ⸢TEe DU₃⸣.DU₃-BI GA₂×GIŠ DU₁₀.GA UGU ⸢KIŠIₓ(DAG.KISIM₅×(LU.GIR₂))-MEŠ⸣

(r 5) You sprinkle sweet oil over the ants [...] their holes. You bury gypsum (and) glasswort in their burrows.

r 66

[x] ⸢pi⸣-il-ši-šu₂-nu ⸢ta?⸣-[sal]-laḫ₃ IM.BABBAR NAGA.SI ina ḪABRUD-⸢šu₂-nu⸣

r 77

te?-[te]-⸢mir⸣ SAḪAR gišMA₂ SAḪAR ⸢u₂⸣-šal-li ID₂ SAḪAR KA₂ <E₂> DINGIR ina ŠEG₃ lu? ina A ⸢PU₂ lu? ina? A? ID₂?⸣

(r 7) You mix dust from a boat, dust from the river meadows (and) dust from the door of a temple in rainwater or in water from a well or in water from a watercourse, and you sprinkle the house (with it).

r 88

ḪI.ḪI-ma E₂ ⸢ta-sal⸣-laḫ₃ NIG₂.NA ⸢šim⸣LI šimSES ina ZA₃.DU₈ ki-lal?-li? GAR-ma ḪUL-šu₂ ⸢DU₈⸣ir

(r 8) You put down censers (full) of juniper (and) myrrh at both door posts and its evil will be released.

r 99

NAM.BUR₂.BI ḪUL ⸢KIŠIₓ(DAG.KISIM₅×(LU.GIR₂))⸣-MEŠ SA₅-MEŠ ša ina ⸢E₂ NA⸣ IGIru

(r 9) Apotropaic ritual for the evil of red ants that have appeared in the house of a man:

r 1010

KA₂ ḪABRUD KI ⸢KIŠIₓ(DAG.KISIM₅×(LU.GIR₂))⸣-MEŠ in-nam-ru [x x] šimLI MU₂

(r 10) You light [...] (full) of juniper at the opening of the burrow where the ants appeared.

r 1111

KAŠ SAG ana ŠA₃ ḪABRUD BALqi₂ UR₅.⸢GIM⸣ DU₁₁.[GA] [d]⸢EREŠ⸣.KI.GAL NIN ša₂?

(r 11) You pour first-class beer into the centre of the burrow. You speak as follows.

(r 11) Ereškigal, lady of the great Underworld, may your messenger that you have sent be good news.

r 1212

KItu₄ GALtu₄ DUMU šip-ri-ki ša₂ ⸢taš⸣-pu-ri

r 1313

lu SIG₅tu₄ a-šar ⸢u₂⸣-ṣu-ni li-tu-ru

(r 13) May they (the ants) return to where they came from.

(colophon)
r 1414

GIM SUMUN-BI ša₃-ṭir-ma ⸢bar₃?-u₃?⸣ ṭup-pi mdMUATI—MU—GAR DUMU mkan-dal₃-a-ni

(r 14) Written according to its original and checked. Tablet of Nabu-šum-iškun, son of Kandalanu.
